title: Review Ledger Row
description: One row of the review ledger (review-ledger-spec.md §1). A ledger file (JSONL) holds two kinds of row, distinguished by the `row_kind` discriminator: a DECISION row written once at gate-decision time (never mutated), and an AMENDMENT row appended later by the back-fill ritual to carry the knowable-later outcome_* group, keyed to a decision row via `outcome_ref_ledger_id`. Append-only integrity is preserved by never editing a decision row; outcomes arrive as separate amendment rows that the metrics reader folds onto their base row at read time (spec §1.3, §2). Modeling choice: `row_kind` is an explicit discriminator added on top of the spec's `outcome_kind="outcome"` marker so a validator can branch cleanly — see boot-integration.md Spec-change notes. SCHEMA v2 (ruling R5, spec §1.1): the canonical `stage` and `unit_kind` enums stay fixed for cross-project comparability, but the first real deployment had to lossy-map project stage/unit-kind names into those enums and could only record the mapping as prose inside `verdict_reason` (invisible to every non-human reader). v2 adds optional `stage_raw` and `unit_kind_raw` on the decision row to preserve the project's own label as data (`outcome_kind_note` on amendment rows was already the same pattern, now ratified into spec §1.1). All v2 fields are optional and `schema_version` keeps a minimum of 1, so every v1 row still validates unchanged.

findingsBySeverity.description: Counts of findings by severity band. All four bands required so absence reads as an explicit zero, not an unknown.

decisionRow.description: A single verdict rendered by one reviewing actor at one pipeline stage on one unit in one rework round. Write-once.

ledger_id.description: Stable unique id, e.g. RL-<seq> or a uuid.

unit_kind_raw.description: Optional (schema_version 2, ruling R5). The project's own unit-kind label, verbatim, when the canonical `unit_kind` map was lossy. Capture rule: map to canonical, preserve raw.

lane.description: Project's lane/team label as a neutral role, not a codename.

stage_raw.description: Optional (schema_version 2, ruling R5). The project's own stage label, verbatim, when the canonical `stage` map was lossy. Capture rule: map to canonical, preserve raw.

reviewer_role.description: Neutral role label (lead, advisor, director, submitting subagent role).

reviewer_model.description: Model/tier label; null for deterministic gates.

finding_ids.description: Stable per-finding ids for later cross-reference.

evidence_verified.description: Null if this stage did not independently re-verify.

wall_clock_seconds.description: Active review duration for this stage.

amendmentRow.description: A knowable-later outcome record, appended by the back-fill ritual / horizon sweep, keyed to a decision row's ledger_id. Never mutates the decision row (spec §1.3, §2).

ledger_id.description: This amendment row's own stable id.

outcome_ref_ledger_id.description: The decision-row ledger_id this outcome amends.

defect_surfaced.description: Did a defect attributable to this unit surface post-merge. Recorded affirmatively as false by the horizon sweep (spec §1.3 step 3).

linked_incident_id.description: Id of the later incident (case-law CL-### / catalog / issue).

escape_stage.description: Which stage should have caught it.

hold_substantiated.description: For hold/fail rows: did the block prove real.

outcome_backfilled_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC, when this amendment was written.
